architecture_fit.note: ReLU-gated bilinear product is the minimal true-AND delta on the dot-product score; sharpness~1 and FPR=0, but the unaddressed high/rising FNR (0.42->0.80) shows the softmax peak misses most A∧B positions.

baseline_comparison.note: Task's additive linear baseline is the explicit strawman; product gates single features off, lift_over_baseline_canonical=0.23 and the Demo radio flips to watch single-feature bars rise.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built synthetic circuit, honestly flagged; causal check is the sum-vs-product swap that collapses the gate, the right ablation for a hardcoded mechanism.

operating_range.note: Full cosine sweep [0,1] with robustness=1.0, but no input-scale/density/noise axis and the steadily worsening FNR is a quiet degradation never surfaced.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set circuit, no training — exactly the by-hand mechanism the bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Clean mean-attention-mass bar chart over the four presence conditions with uniform line, baseline-flip radio and cosine slider makes the AND claim legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies the y-axis (attention mass), the four conditions, the baseline flip and the cosine slider, tying each directly to the AND/superposition question.

notes: A clean hand-built ReLU-gated bilinear AND head that is the minimal nonlinear delta from base_model's attention score, beats the additive baseline, and holds sharpness=1 / FPR=0 across the full cosine sweep (robustness=1.0). The main weakness is the high and rising false-negative rate (0.42->0.80), an artifact of the softmax peak concentrating on one A∧B position that the attempt never addresses or surfaces in its operating-range story. Strong visualisation and rationale, and the fully hardcoded circuit earns the by-hand bonus.
